title: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure with Missing Headings

statement.markdown: Given a string representing an HTML table structure, fix the bug that prevents the table from being parsed correctly when it has missing table headers. The function should return the corrected HTML string. Note that missing table headers may be at the top of the table or within the table rows. The corrected HTML string should have table headers present in all rows. If a table row does not have a header, the function should add a default header to that row. The default header should be a placeholder string 'Header Not Found'.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing an HTML table structure. The string may contain nested HTML tags and attributes.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected HTML string with table headers present in all rows.
statement.notes: The corrected HTML string should not have any syntax errors and should be valid HTML 5.

examples.explanation: The table has missing headers, so the function adds a default header to each row.
The table row does not have a header, so the function adds a default header to that row.

hints.text: Use the DOM parser to parse the HTML string and extract the table structure

starterCode.javascript: function fixTable(htmlString) { // implement the function here }

functionSignature.javascript: function fixTable(htmlString) { // implement the function here }
